source.definition: to be situated in a particular place or to be in a certain state or condition

explanation.sourceText: The verb "locate" comes from the Latin word "locare" meaning "to place" or "to position." It entered English through Old French and is used to indicate the act of finding or establishing the position of something.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „locate” provine din latină, de la „locare”, care înseamnă „a plasa” sau „a poziționa”. A intrat în limba engleză prin franceza veche și este folosit pentru a indica actul de a găsi sau stabili poziția unui lucru.

source.additional: Regular verb. Commonly used to describe the position or condition of something.

target.definition: a fi într-un anumit loc sau într-o anumită stare

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian expression „a se afla“ derives from the Latin „afflare“ (to be located, to be present) and means "to be situated."
explanation.targetText: Expresia românească „a se afla“ provine din latină, de la „afflare“, cu sensul de „a se găsi“ sau „a fi prezent“.

target.additional: Reflexive verb of the 1st conjugation. Frequently used to describe the state or position of a person or thing.

explanation.sourceText: Used to talk about the state of a person or thing.
explanation.targetText: Folosit pentru a indica starea unei persoane sau a unui obiect.

examples.targetText: El se află într-o stare proastă de sănătate.

senses.gloss: To be located / to stay at a specific place

explanation.sourceText: Used to describe a specific location.
explanation.targetText: Folosit pentru a descrie o locație specifică.

examples.targetText: El se afla adesea în străinătate.

examples.sourceText: I will be in the office.

learningTips.text: Remember that "a se afla" means to be located or to be in a state. Think of the English word "affair" which sounds similar and involves a situation or state.
The Romanian verb "a se afla" is reflexive, similar to English phrases like "to find oneself" in a place or condition.
Link the sound "afla" with the English word "find" since both relate to discovering or locating something.
Practice the forms: "Mă aflu" (I am located), "M-am aflat" (I was located), "Mă voi afla" (I will be located) to get comfortable with reflexive verbs.
